$comment: Repo-local config for the spec/dev/qa board sweeps. Copy to <target-repo>/.claude/linear-sweep.json and fill in. Nothing in the skills is hardcoded — they read this file.
projectId: <project uuid — get it from: node scripts/linear.mjs ensure-project "<team>" "<project>">
issuePrefix: <team key used in card ids, e.g. COD>
$comment_repos: Repo scope is ship scope. Default to one deployable repo per card. For true multi-repo cards, list every sibling repo the sweeps may touch and make deploy/canary instructions cover every production target; otherwise split work into per-repo cards. Scheduled dispatch clones these repos into a managed workspace under ~/.local/share/linear-board-sweeps/workspaces/<anchor>/; source checkout dirtiness is advisory but unpushed local commits are invisible to sweeps.
repos: <repo folder name(s) the sweeps may touch>
$comment_repoRouting: Optional for single-repo workspaces; required when one Linear project owns cards across multiple primary repos. Each key is an exact Linear label and each value must exactly equal one repos entry. A scheduled card must carry exactly one mapped label or it fails closed before claim/spawn. Multiple mapped labels are ambiguous even when they target the same repo.
$comment_runtime: Scheduled sweep runtime selection. Prefer runtimes.<sweep> for per-stage dispatch. Legacy runtime + models remain as a fallback when runtimes.<sweep> is absent. The review entry is a reviewer role preference for sweep instructions, not a scheduled stage.
$comment_parallel: Optional repo-local demand limits beneath the host ceiling. maxNonShipDispatches defaults to 2, counts selected non-Ship workspace/sweep candidates, and caps follow-up handoff dispatches per parent tick. Ship is one child per source workspace, may coexist with every stage, and refills its workspace slot after completion without bypassing the human Ship gate. sameRepoCardLimits defaults to spec/dev=4, qa=1 and counts active child slots inside each selected non-Ship candidate. maxSameRepoRefillDispatches defaults to 8, clamps 0..20, and caps child slots backfilled after successful completion; 0 disables refill. maxHandoffTriggerHops defaults to 2 and controls immediate spec->dev/dev->qa follow-ups for the same card; 0 disables. maxDrainPasses defaults to 5 and is clamped to 1..5, so a tick can re-check queues up to four times after dispatched passes. Set maxNonShipDispatches, maxSameRepoRefillDispatches, or maxDrainPasses to 1 for stricter bounded mode; Ship is never handoff-triggered.
$comment_host_capacity: The launcher registry, not this repo-local file, owns host-wide capacity.maxActiveChildren (default 10, clamp 1..32). It caps initial, refill, and handoff top-level scheduled children across all registered anchors and launcher restarts. Reviewer subagents inside a scheduled child are not counted; parallel settings below only shape demand beneath that ceiling.
$comment_learning: Factory Learning is disabled by default per workspace. When enabled, the single registry-pinned learning runner observes this workspace even if delivery auto-sweeps are paused. Reliability, quality/rework, and throughput/cost lenses use bounded structured evidence; medium/high findings create or update factory:learning-generated cards at the bottom of Spec. Generated cards always require QA -> Signoff and a human move to Ship and never use the auto-ship marker.
$comment_reviewLenses: Optional. Card-type gating for review lenses: a card carrying any of a lens's labels fires that lens (design at spec+qa, devex at spec, security at spec+dev+ship, perf at dev/qa, research at spec). Omit the whole block to run only the always-on lenses (eng-review, code-review, /qa). setup-team creates these labels; sweeps auto-apply them to unlabelled cards (generate-if-missing).
$comment_requireShipApproval: Optional (default false). When true, qa-sweep always preserves Signoff and ship-sweep requires a human-applied `ship:approved` label in addition to the card being in 'Ship' before it will merge — a second deliberate act that hardens against an accidental bulk-drag. (The single-runner ship pin lives in the launcher registry: `node scripts/linear-watch.mjs ship-runner on`.)
$comment_fastPath: Enabled by default. Dev-sweep may mark a tiny, high-confidence, low-risk change only after its pushed full origin SHA matches the reviewed final commit. Commit-bound QA-to-Ship automatic routing occurs after full QA only when that SHA is unchanged and requireShipApproval is false. factory:learning-generated cards never use the auto-ship marker and always preserve Signoff for the human Ship move. Normal denials, enabled false, and requireShipApproval true preserve Signoff. qa-sweep never merges or deploys, no immediate QA-to-Ship launcher handoff is added, and ship-sweep remains single-runner.

canonicalDocs.$comment: Single-repo projects can point architecture at CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md and leave schema null. Multi-repo setups name a canonical schema.md/architecture.md pair.

deploy: <how this project reaches production for every repo in config.repos — e.g. 'web: Vercel auto-deploys on push to main; api: run ...; manual DB migration Todo required before deploy'>
$comment_artifacts: Scheduled sweeps receive AUTO_SWEEP_ANCHOR for the managed config owner, AUTO_SWEEP_REPO and AUTO_SWEEP_SOURCE_REPO for the label-routed primary repo, AUTO_SWEEP_REPO_LABEL and AUTO_SWEEP_REPO_ENTRY for the mandatory live child preflight, AUTO_SWEEP_WORKTREE beneath its managed clone, plus AUTO_SWEEP_LOG_DIR, AUTO_SWEEP_TMPDIR, AUTO_SWEEP_SCREENSHOT_DIR, and AUTO_SWEEP_BROWSER_PROFILE_DIR under state/cache paths. Evidence and scratch artifacts should use those paths rather than repo roots.
credentialsNote: <where app + tooling secrets live — e.g. '.env.local for app secrets; Linear key in gitignored .env copied to managed clones with mode 0600'>
